names.thesis: Kaiser Aluminum delivered a very strong Q1 2026 (revenue +42% YoY, EPS beat, record EBITDA, margins up ~850bps) helped by tariff protection and aerospace demand, and Zacks flags it as a beneficiary of Section-232-style cost/tariff dynamics in metal fabrication. However Wells Fargo just downgraded to underweight (PT cut to $158) on valuation after the run-up, and as a fabricator KALU largely passes through aluminum costs rather than capturing upside directly, consistent with the desk's SHORT-lean aluminum view. Q2 earnings land July 22-23, 2026 as the next catalyst.
catalysts: Q2 2026 earnings/call July 22-23, 2026
Tariff-driven aerospace/auto demand strength
Continued margin improvement from facility performance gains
risks: Wells Fargo downgrade (underweight, PT $158) flags valuation risk after 2026 rally
Fabricator model passes through aluminum price moves rather than capturing them
Elevated input costs (steel, aluminum, freight, labor) industry-wide per Zacks

evidence.note: Wells Fargo downgraded KALU to underweight from equal weight, PT cut to $158 from $160; shares fell >2%
Q1 2026 revenue +42% YoY, EPS beat $1.78, record EBITDA, margins up ~850bps YoY, FCF $69M, liquidity ~$596M
Kaiser Aluminum to report Q2 2026 results July 22, 2026 with call July 23

names.thesis: Metallus is a clean read-through on the desk's Steel LONG thesis: Section 232 tariffs continue to insulate its primary steel products (April 2026 tariff updates hit only downstream derivatives, not Metallus' core products), order book grew YoY on industrial/defense demand and onshoring, and the company has pushed through bar/tube price actions ($120/ton and $100/ton respectively) while new bloom reheat and roller furnace capacity comes online through Q3 2026. Simply Wall St flags the tariff-support/onshoring narrative but also notes cyclical end-market and leverage risk via its asset-backed credit facility.
catalysts: New bloom reheat furnace (150 tons/hr vs 100 legacy) fully operational early-to-mid Q3 2026; roller furnace late Q3
Bar/tube price actions flowing through non-contract volume (~30% of annual volume) in H2 2026
Continued Section 232 tariff protection on primary steel products
risks: Heavy reliance on cyclical industrial end markets
High fixed costs and leverage via asset-backed credit facility
Exposure to tariffs on certain imported inputs despite core-product protection

evidence.note: Q1 2026 earnings call: shipments +11% sequentially, order book grew YoY, Section 232 tariffs continue to support competitive position
Metallus fits US onshoring story as domestic steel producer benefiting from tariffs making imports less competitive; Q1 2026 higher sales/earnings plus buybacks

names.thesis: Antofagasta is a strong fit for the desk's Copper LONG thesis: JPMorgan just upgraded to overweight, raising 2026/2027 copper price forecasts (+2%/+15%) and group EBITDA forecasts (+3%/+24%), citing Centinela's second concentrator project on time/budget and 10-30% copper growth potential by 2027-28 with lower relative risk than peers. Berenberg similarly raised its price target to £44 from £37 ahead of H1 results (Aug 13, 2026), though it flags Q2 as another H2-weighted production year and stays Hold-rated on valuation (2.65x NAV).
catalysts: Q2 2026 production report due July 15, 2026
H1 2026 results August 13, 2026
Centinela second concentrator ramp supporting 10-30% copper growth by 2027-28
30% production growth target by 2030 announced at World Mining Congress 2026
risks: H2-weighted production skew creates near-term volume softness (Los Pelambres grade constraints)
Berenberg remains Hold on valuation (2.65x NAV, 9.2x 2026E EBITDA)
Chile-concentrated single-country operational risk

analyst.Berenberg: Hold, PT raised to GBP44 from GBP37, 2026-06-19

evidence.note: JPMorgan raises ANTO to overweight, copper price forecasts and EBITDA estimates raised, EPS 2026E to $1.96 from $1.79
Berenberg raises PT to GBP44 from GBP37, expects H2-weighted 2026 production, Hold rating
Antofagasta targets 30% production increase by 2030 per CEO Ivan Arriagada at World Mining Congress

names.thesis: Ivanhoe fits the Copper LONG thesis on a recovery arc: Kamoa-Kakula copper output is set to rise in H2 2026 as mining rates increase and inventories are drawn down, positioning it as a key source of global copper supply growth into a tighter 2026 market. However, this follows an April 2026 cut to 2026/2027 output guidance after seismic disruption at Kakula, and revised cash cost guidance ($2.60-3.00/lb for 2026) reflects the lower-production, higher-cost base — so the thesis is a rebound off a reset floor rather than unambiguous strength.
catalysts: H2 2026 ramp in Kamoa-Kakula mining rates and inventory drawdown
Medium-term cash cost target of ~$2.00/lb from 2028 as smelter efficiencies mature
Global copper market tightening into 2026 per analyst forecasts
risks: April 2026 guidance cut for 2026/2027 output following seismic disruption at Kakula
Elevated 2026/2027 cash costs ($2.60-3.00/lb) vs. medium-term target
Execution risk on capex deferral and mill utilization ramp

evidence.note: Ivanhoe says Kamoa-Kakula copper output set to rise in H2 2026; company cut 2026/2027 output forecasts in April after seismic disruption at Kakula
2026/2027 cash cost guidance revised to $2.60-3.00/lb (2026) reflecting lower production forecast and grade; medium-term target ~$2.00/lb from 2028

names.thesis: Siemens Energy is riding record order momentum in Grid Technologies (electrical-steel-intensive transformers/grid equipment) driven by AI data-center power demand — Q2 group orders hit an all-time high of EUR17.75bn with backlog at EUR154bn, and Berenberg/BofA have both raised estimates and price targets (Buy-rated) into the fiscal Q3 report, citing above-guidance Grid margins and double-digit order upside. As a downstream consumer of electrical steel rather than a producer, ENR's exposure is to volume/margin capture from grid buildout rather than direct material price risk.
catalysts: Fiscal Q3 2026 results expected to show 'solid' Gas and Grid order strength (BofA)
Mid-term target update expected at full-year results November 11, 2026
Possible new share buyback announcement (more likely November per BofA)
risks: Gamesa (offshore wind) weakness offsetting Gas/Grid strength (no offshore orders expected in Q3)
'Peak order' concerns among investors that Q3 results need to reassure against
Electrical steel input cost inflation could pressure Grid Technologies margins

evidence.note: BofA expects solid fiscal Q3 on Gas/Grid upside, PT raised to EUR260 from EUR250, Buy maintained
Q2 group orders at all-time high EUR17.75bn, backlog EUR154bn, FY26 FCF guidance already delivered in H1 alone

names.thesis: Franco-Nevada's royalty/streaming model continues to outperform on record gold prices — Q1 2026 adjusted EPS of $2.38 beat consensus ($2.11) on revenue of $650.7M (up from $368.4M YoY), and CIBC named it its top pick for precious metals exposure with a C$480 target after its Ontario mine tour. Consistent with the desk's view that royalty companies score higher than operators even as gold is SHORT-medium overall, FNV's insulated cost structure (revenues largely unaffected by input cost inflation) and 2026 GEO guidance (510-570koz) support a defensive-but-not-aggressive stance.
catalysts: Cobre Panama stockpile-ore processing approval adding incremental GEO deliveries (mostly 2027)
Ontario portfolio growth (Porcupine/Cote/Detour Lake/Island Gold) highlighted on CIBC mine tour
Continued M&A/deal pipeline deployment in Tier-1 jurisdictions
risks: Gold price is the desk's SHORT-medium call; a pullback compresses royalty NAV even without operating leverage
Cobre Panama GEO contribution remains 'relatively moderate' in 2026, back-half loaded
New independent chair transition (Tom Albanese) is a governance watch item

evidence.note: Q1 2026 adjusted EPS $2.38 vs $1.07 YoY, beat consensus $2.11; revenue $650.7M vs $368.4M YoY
CIBC reiterates Outperform, PT C$480, calls FNV 'top pick for precious metals exposure'
2026 GEO sales guidance maintained at 510,000-570,000 oz, excluding Cobre Panama upside

names.thesis: Royal Gold posted record Q1 2026 results (revenue $469.1M, +142.5% YoY; 83% adjusted EBITDA margin) on record realized gold ($4,873/oz) and silver ($84.33/oz) prices plus the first full-quarter contribution from the Sandstorm Gold and Horizon Copper acquisitions, consolidating the mid-tier royalty space. RBC trimmed its PT slightly to $305 (from $310) but maintains Outperform, and consensus remains Overweight — consistent with the desk's view that royalty names deserve a higher score than operating miners even with gold SHORT-medium overall.
catalysts: 2026 guidance tracking within range (gold 290-320koz, silver 3.0-3.5Moz, copper 21-25M lbs) through Q1
Hod Maden project restructuring expected to close H2 2026, preserving ~4% NAV exposure with reduced capex risk
Full integration of Sandstorm/Horizon Copper deals expanding royalty base
risks: Gold desk view is SHORT-medium; elevated realized prices ($4,873/oz) create high bar for continued outperformance
RGLD down 5.4% since last earnings report despite record results, signaling market skepticism
Turkish regulatory approval still pending for Hod Maden restructuring

evidence.note: Q1 2026 revenue $469.1M (+142.5% YoY), record operating cash flow $293.6M, net income $281.1M, EBITDA margin 83%
RBC adjusts PT to $305 from $310, maintains Outperform; consensus average rating overweight, mean PT $320.08
2026 guidance reiterated: gold 290-320koz, silver 3.0-3.5Moz, copper 21-25M lbs, tracking within range

names.thesis: Giyani Metals is a pre-production developer, not a current earner — it just filed the full NI 43-101 DFS for its 100%-owned K.Hill battery-grade manganese project in Botswana, showing a post-tax NPV of $482M and 20.3% IRR, with project start targeted for April 2027. Manganese is not part of the desk's explicit metal coverage (copper/gold/silver/RE/zinc-tin/nickel/aluminum/steel/moly), and with no near-term production or revenue, this is a battery-materials optionality/financing story rather than a 2026 earnings or price-exposure trade.
catalysts: DFS filing complete, advancing project toward financing/construction decision
Battery-grade manganese demand tailwind from next-gen battery chemistries
Project targeted to start production April 2027
risks: Pre-revenue TSXV micro-cap with financing/dilution risk ahead of construction decision
No near-term (2026) production or earnings to benchmark against price moves
Manganese pricing/demand not covered by desk's current commodity backdrop

evidence.note: DFS filed for K.Hill Battery-Grade Manganese Project: post-tax NPV $482M at 8% discount, IRR 20.3%, project start April 2027

names.thesis: Western Mining pre-announced H1 2026 net profit up 114-130% YoY (to RMB4.0-4.3bn), explicitly attributed to higher copper, gold and silver product prices rather than molybdenum, which the desk scores NEUTRAL. Shares jumped 4-7% on the news as part of a broader A-share copper-sector rally (Jiangxi Copper, Luoyang Molybdenum also up), so the near-term catalyst is more copper/precious-metals beta than a moly-specific signal, though Western Mining's diversified base-and-precious-metals portfolio benefits regardless.
catalysts: H1 2026 profit guidance confirms 114-130% YoY growth on metals price strength
Broader China copper-sector rally on confirmed price bottom
Diversified copper/gold/silver exposure buffers single-commodity risk
risks: Molybdenum-specific exposure (desk's actual coverage angle) not the primary driver of recent strength
State-owned parent restructuring (Xinjiang Nonferrous merger) is a governance event to monitor, though company says no operational impact
P/E already re-rated to ~11.4-12x TTM on the earnings beat, limiting further re-rating room

evidence.note: H1 2026 net profit guided +114% to +130% YoY (RMB4.0-4.3bn) on higher copper, gold, silver prices; shares +7%
Copper sector surged 2.5% on confirmed price bottom; Western Mining up >4% alongside Jiangxi Copper, Luoyang Molybdenum

names.thesis: Alleima continues to win specialty orders (a SEK995M umbilical tube order for Indonesia's Kutei North Hub project, deliveries through 2030) and inaugurated a new Tube Mill 2026 targeting nuclear-power demand, but the available evidence lacks any near-term margin or stainless/nickel price commentary ahead of its Q2 report (due July 17, 2026). This is consistent with the desk's NEUTRAL-bearish stance on nickel/stainless given seasonal off-season dynamics — order wins are encouraging longer-term but not an immediate 2026 earnings catalyst.
catalysts: Q2 2026 interim report and webcast July 17, 2026
SEK995M subsea umbilical tube order supports multi-year backlog visibility
New Tube Mill 2026 capacity aligned with nuclear power demand growth
risks: Stainless/nickel seasonal off-season per desk backdrop could pressure near-term volumes/pricing
No evidence found of updated margin or raw material cost guidance ahead of Q2 print
Order backlog delivery stretches to 2030, limiting near-term revenue recognition

evidence.note: Alleima secures SEK995M order for umbilical tubes for Indonesia's Kutei North Hub project, deliveries through 2030
Alleima Q2 2026 interim report scheduled July 17, 2026

names.thesis: Sherritt is facing acute company-specific disruption layered on top of the desk's NEUTRAL-bearish nickel view: the May 1, 2026 US Executive Order expanding Cuba sanctions forced a suspension of mining at the Moa JV in February, driving Q1 2026 finished nickel production down 36% YoY and cobalt down 34%, and the resulting filing delay triggered an OSC cease-trade order (revoked July 10, 2026, trading resumed) — a governance/liquidity scare on top of weak fundamentals.
catalysts: Cease trade order revoked July 10, 2026, trading resumed on TSX
Potential recovery in Moa JV output if Cuban raw material supply normalizes
risks: Cuba sanctions expansion (May 2026 Executive Order) directly disrupted raw material supply and production
Q1 2026 nickel/cobalt production and sales down 34-36% YoY
Regulatory/governance risk evidenced by cease-trade order for late filing

evidence.note: Q1 2026 finished nickel production down 36% YoY to 1,885 tonnes; cobalt down 34% to 213 tonnes, due to Moa JV mining suspension from Cuban supply shortage
OSC cease trade order issued May 21, 2026 after Sherritt failed to file Q1 2026 statements on time, citing disruption from May 1 US Executive Order expanding Cuba sanctions
OSC revoked the failure-to-file cease trade order; TSX trading resumed

names.thesis: Avalon sits on a strategically significant North American rare earth resource (Nechalacho) and is advancing a PEA update to its 2013 DFS, which aligns directionally with the desk's Rare Earths LONG thesis (NdPr +74% YoY). However, the dominant recent news is a 180:1 share consolidation completed July 8, 2026 after shares fell to $0.06 — a balance-sheet/capital-structure distress signal ahead of a potential US listing bid, not a fundamentals-driven catalyst, so this remains a pre-revenue, dilution-risk story rather than a direct way to play RE price strength.
catalysts: 180:1 share consolidation completed, intended to support a future US exchange listing
Nechalacho PEA update advancing, updating the 2013 DFS on North America's most strategically significant RE resource
Sector tailwind from NdPr price strength (+74% YoY per desk backdrop)
risks: 180:1 reverse split reflects severe share price deterioration (traded at $0.06 pre-consolidation) and dilution history
Pre-revenue development stage; no production or near-term earnings to capture RE price upside
Contingent on future US capital markets access, not yet secured

evidence.note: Avalon completed 180:1 common share consolidation, effective July 8, 2026, to support potential future US exchange listing
Avalon provides YTD progress update on Nechalacho Rare Earth Elements and Zirconium Project, advancing PEA update to 2013 DFS

names.thesis: Fresnillo shows the tension in the desk's SHORT-low silver call: Berenberg expects flat Q2 volumes and an H1 revenue beat (USD3.6bn vs consensus USD3.3bn) but trimmed its price target to £33 from £34 on a stronger Mexican peso as a currency headwind, while Simply Wall St flags that Q1 2026 silver and gold production volumes were actually lower than the prior year despite strong margins (30.3% net margin) as an inflation-hedge play. Zacks upgraded to Buy on rising estimates, but the underlying volume softness and hold-rated valuation support a cautious stance.
catalysts: Zacks upgrade to Buy with consensus estimates raised 10.9% over past three months
Sinda minority stake acquisition (up to 5%) expanding silver-gold district exposure in Mexico ahead of Sinda's NYSE IPO
H1 2026 revenue expected to beat consensus per Berenberg
risks: Q1 2026 silver and gold production volumes lower YoY despite price tailwinds
Berenberg cut PT to GBP33 from GBP34 on stronger MXN as FX headwind, maintains Hold
Aligns with desk's SHORT-low silver view given production softness and currency drag

evidence.note: Berenberg forecasts broadly flat Q2 volumes, H1 revenue beat (USD3.6bn vs cons USD3.3bn), but cuts PT to GBP33 from GBP34 on MXN strength, Hold maintained
Q1 2026 silver and gold production volumes lower than prior year despite ~30.3% net margins and inflation-hedge appeal

names.thesis: Yunnan Tin is a strong direct play on the desk's Zinc/Tin LONG (tin bullish) thesis, benefiting from both tin price strength and an emerging AI-driven indium demand story (indium phosphide substrate prices surged from $800 to $2,500 within a year); 2025 revenue rose 3.7% to RMB43.5bn with net profit up 36.1%, and growth accelerated further in Q1 2026. Dongwu Securities explicitly recommends the tin sector on supply constraints, though the company's structural weakness ('large-scale smelting, small-scale mining') means it relies heavily on externally purchased raw material, pressuring operating cash flow via higher prepayments and margin deposits.
catalysts: AI/data-center-driven indium phosphide demand (indium demand forecast to grow from 19t in 2025 to 419t by 2030)
Sector-wide tin price strength on supply constraints (Dongwu Securities 'spring of tin, indium, hafnium' thesis)
Q1 2026 revenue/profit growth accelerating vs. FY2025
risks: Structural reliance on externally purchased ore ('large-scale smelting, small-scale mining') pressures cash flow via prepayments/margin deposits
2025 operating cash flow fell 62% YoY despite profit growth, a working-capital red flag
China smelting capacity concentration risk

evidence.note: 2025 revenue RMB43.535bn (+3.7% YoY), net profit RMB1.966bn (+36.1% YoY); Q1 2026 growth accelerated further; operating cash flow fell 62% YoY on higher prepayments/margin deposits

Dongwu Securities recommends tin sector (incl. Yunnan Tin) citing supply-side constraints expected to keep mid-price of tin rising; indium phosphide demand for AI data centers to drive indium demand growth >20%

names.thesis: Minsur benefits from the desk's tin-bullish thesis (LME tin ~$53,450) even as its own Q1 2026 refined tin output from the Pisco smelter fell 2.9% YoY — supply softness at Minsur plus disruptions at Renison (Tasmania, -13% QoQ) and the DRC's Bisie mine reinforces low global tin stocks and price support, with BMI/Sucden Financial noting the market stays vulnerable to renewed squeezes into H2 2026 on Indonesia/Myanmar supply risk. IMPORTANT: MINSURI1 trades on the Lima Stock Exchange and is very likely NOT tradable via IBKR — flag this as an execution/tradability constraint before sizing any position.
catalysts: Global tin stocks low and declining on LME/SHFE, keeping the market exposed to squeezes
Semiconductor-industry demand expected to keep tin prices elevated through 2026
Minsur's RDP copper-gold exploration investment (Pacific Ridge, BC) diversifies beyond tin
risks: NOT tradable via IBKR (sanctioned/foreign-exchange listing)
TRADABILITY: Lima-listed (MINSURI1) — likely not accessible via IBKR; confirm broker access before any trade
Minsur's own Pisco smelter output declined 2.9% YoY in Q1 2026, an idiosyncratic supply miss even as prices firm
Indonesia/Myanmar supply risk cuts both ways — could also ease tightness if resolved

evidence.note: Minsur Q1 2026 refined tin output from Pisco smelter down 2.9% YoY to 8,314 tonnes; global tin stocks low on LME/SHFE, market vulnerable to squeezes into H2 2026
Minsur invests in Pacific Ridge Exploration's RDP copper-gold project in British Columbia, diversifying beyond core tin business
